bead.title: beads: sort ready queues by queue-rank within priority
bead.description:
PROBLEM
FEAT-004 and SD-004 now require `queue-rank` to order ready beads within a priority bucket, but the store still ignores queue-rank. Operators currently have to change `priority` to force a bead to the top, which changes urgency semantics and can reorder unrelated work.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:104-119 defines canonical ready-queue ordering as priority, queue-rank, created_at, id, and says queue-rank must not cross priority or bypass execution filters.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-004-beads-tracker.md:196-209 defines the same deterministic queue derivation and numeric compatibility rules.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1346-1355 exposes Ready and ReadyExecution, but both delegate to readyFiltered without queue-rank-specific behavior.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1702-1711 sorts only by Priority, CreatedAt, and ID.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a small queue-rank parser/helper in cli/internal/bead (acceptable names: QueueRank, queueRank, or parseQueueRank) that accepts integer values and numeric strings from Bead.Extra["queue-rank"].
- Update sortBeadsForQueue to sort by priority asc, explicit queue-rank asc with missing rank after explicit rank inside the same priority, created_at asc, then id asc.
- Keep ReadyExecution filtering unchanged: blocked, cooldown, superseded, execution-eligible=false, and epic-only beads must be excluded before sorting.
- Add focused store tests in cli/internal/bead/gaps_test.go or queue-specific test file.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add queue-rank as a Bead struct field or known schema field.
- Do not implement `ddx bead queue` CLI commands in this bead.
- Do not change priority validation, dependency semantics, or claim semantics.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestReadySortsByQueueRankWithinPriority verifies an explicit lower `queue-rank` sorts before unranked and higher-ranked beads of the same priority.
2. TestReadyExecutionSortsByQueueRankWithinPriority verifies ReadyExecution uses the same queue-rank order after execution-ready filtering.
3. TestQueueRankDoesNotCrossPriorityBoundary verifies a ranked P1 bead never sorts before an unranked P0 bead.
4. TestQueueRankMissingSortsAfterExplicitRankWithinPriority verifies missing queue-rank sorts after explicit ranks in the same priority bucket.
5. TestQueueRankNumericStringCompatibility verifies numeric-string queue-rank values are read compatibly.
6. WIRED-IN: sortBeadsForQueue, used by Ready and ReadyExecution, calls the queue-rank helper before created_at/id tie-breakers.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -run "TestReady.*QueueRank|TestQueueRank" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
